dashboard.label: Configuration
dashboard.description: Configure default settings for Webex Alert Action

div.style: padding: 20px; background: #0078D4; color: white; border-radius: 5px; margin: 10px;
div.p: To set default values for your Webex alert action, use the manual configuration method below:
This is the recommended method to configure default values for all Webex alerts.

pre.style: background: white; color: black; padding: 15px; margin: 10px 0; border-radius: 5px; font-family: monospace;

div.pre:
# 1. Create local directory if it doesn't exist mkdir -p /opt/splunk/etc/apps/webex_alert/local
# 2. Create or edit the config file cat > /opt/splunk/etc/apps/webex_alert/local/alert_actions.conf <<EOF [webex_alert] param.bot_token = YOUR_BOT_TOKEN_HERE param.room_id = YOUR_ROOM_NAME_OR_ID param.message_type = text EOF
# 3. Fix permissions chown -R splunk:splunk /opt/splunk/etc/apps/webex_alert/local/
# 4. Restart Splunk /opt/splunk/bin/splunk restart

div.style: background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); padding: 12px; margin-top: 15px; border-radius: 3px; color: white;

ul.li: With defaults configured: All new alerts automatically use these values
Without defaults: You must configure bot token and room ID for each alert

search.query: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/webex_alert/configs/conf-alert_actions/webex_alert splunk_server=local | eval bot_token_val = 'param.bot_token' | eval room_id_val = 'param.room_id' | eval message_type_val = 'param.message_type' | eval bot_token_status = if(isnotnull(bot_token_val) AND len(bot_token_val)>10, "✅ Configured (***" . substr(bot_token_val, -4, 4) . ")", "❌ Not Configured") | eval room_id_status = if(isnotnull(room_id_val) AND len(room_id_val)>0, "✅ " . room_id_val, "❌ Not Configured") | eval message_type_status = if(isnotnull(message_type_val) AND message_type_val!="", "✅ " . message_type_val, "⚠️ Default (text)") | table bot_token_status, room_id_status, message_type_status | rename bot_token_status AS "Bot Token", room_id_status AS "Room Name/ID", message_type_status AS "Message Type"

ul.li: Configuration File: Settings are saved to /opt/splunk/etc/apps/webex_alert/local/alert_actions.conf
Auto-Fill: When you create a new alert, these default values will automatically appear in the Webex Message action form
Per-Alert Override: You can change the bot token or room for any specific alert
Security: Bot tokens are stored in Splunk's configuration files with restricted access

ol.li: Check the "Current Configuration Status" panel above - it should show ✅ for all fields
Create a test alert: Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts → New Alert
Check if bot token and room fields are pre-filled
